claimText: A Pangenome is a collection of genome sequences from multiple individuals of the same species.
evidenceExcerpt: A Pangenome is a collection of genome sequences from many individuals of the same species.
examUse: Statement-based question distinguishing a pangenome from a traditional single reference genome.
distractorHooks: A collection of genomes from different interacting species
A single, highly accurate reference genome of one individual

claimText: PacBio HiFi sequencing is a single-molecule, real-time (SMRT) long-read sequencing method.
evidenceExcerpt: PacBio HiFi sequencing is a single-molecule, real-time (SMRT) long-read sequencing method.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on modern sequencing technologies and their characteristics.
distractorHooks: A method that requires extensive PCR amplification prior to sequencing

claimText: Pangenomes can be used to introduce disease-tolerance traits in crops and identify population-specific genetic markers in humans.
evidenceExcerpt: Pangenomes help introduce disease-tolerance traits in crops and identify population-specific genetic markers in humans.
examUse: Application-based question on the utility of pangenome sequencing in agriculture and human genetics.
distractorHooks: Pangenomes are exclusively used for human genetic mapping
Pangenomes cannot identify population-specific markers due to high genetic variation
